why_the_dock_is_a_co_equal_product.statement: Four of the system's five distinguishing claims depend on the dock, not the aircraft. It is treated in most drone pitches as an accessory. Here it is the thing that makes the product a product.

the_four_dependencies.claim: We measure the pre-dawn dissolved oxygen minimum
the_four_dependencies.depends_on: Autonomous launch. A drone that needs a human to launch it will never fly at 04:30, which means it only ever measures the safest hour of the day — precisely the failure it was built to fix.

the_four_dependencies.claim: Our probe fouls less than a moored buoy
the_four_dependencies.depends_on: The dock rinse cycle. Without it we are just a slower buoy with worse uptime.

the_four_dependencies.claim: Our multispectral time series is comparable across days
the_four_dependencies.depends_on: The calibrated reflectance panel living in the dock. Without it, every survey is a separate uncalibrated snapshot.

the_four_dependencies.claim: We operate across sites without spreading pathogens

why_the_dock_is_a_co_equal_product.the_fifth: Depth profiling is the only claim that belongs purely to the aircraft.
why_the_dock_is_a_co_equal_product.the_business_consequence: Coverage is measured in docks, not drones. The dock is the unit of network economics, the unit of capital deployment, and the thing a customer actually installs. Price and plan accordingly.

functions.detail: Protects the aircraft from sun, rain, and salt spray between sorties. In tropical UV and monsoon rain, an unsheltered aircraft degrades fast.
Recharge or battery swap. See power-energy-budget.json — this decision is open and it determines sortie cadence.
Freshwater rinse of the sonde AND the tether after every sortie. The tether is the part everyone forgets, and wiping the sonde while reeling in a contaminated line accomplishes nothing.
Chemical decontamination cycle appropriate to the pathogens of concern before any inter-site movement.
Houses the calibrated reflectance panel imaged before and after every imaging sortie, and a sonde reference-check standard.
The site's connectivity point. Cellular primary, with local storage and store-and-forward for when the link is down — which it will be.
Local processing and storage so the site works when the cloud does not.
The aircraft is a valuable, portable asset sitting unattended on a lakeshore. This is not a hypothetical concern.

the_thermal_problem.statement: A closed box in tropical sun exceeds the battery's safe storage temperature. This degrades pack life substantially and silently.
the_thermal_problem.why_it_is_here: It is a dock requirement generated by an airframe constraint — exactly the cross-subsystem coupling that gets missed when subsystems are designed independently.
mitigations: Passive ventilation with insect and vermin screening
Active cooling if the arithmetic demands it, at a power cost the solar budget must carry
the_thermal_problem.note: Getting this wrong does not produce a dramatic failure. It produces batteries that quietly last half as long as the business model assumed, discovered a year in, in the operating cost line.

siting_requirements.detail: Reachable by a technician on foot or by short boat trip. If servicing the dock is an expedition, it will not be serviced.
Grid where available; solar-plus-storage as the design case. Rural sites are the design case, not the edge case.
Cellular preferred. Store-and-forward mandatory regardless — the link will fail and the pre-dawn data must survive it.
No power lines, masts, or trees on the launch and recovery path. Surveyed at commissioning.

siting_requirements.requirement: Within operating radius of the stations
siting_requirements.detail: 800 m target, constrained by return-with-reserve rather than by radio range.
Someone owns the shore. That someone must agree, in writing, and they may not be the farm's owner.
A dock is a permanent, visible installation on a shoreline where people live. Siting it without talking to those people is how a company loses its social licence before it has a customer.

solar_and_storage.basis: Four sorties/day at ~220 Wh plus charging losses plus dock systems.
solar_and_storage.the_monsoon_problem: Sizing for average insolation is easy. Sizing for an extended overcast monsoon stretch is expensive — days of storage, or accept degraded cadence. And the degraded cadence lands exactly during the season of highest fish kill risk.
solar_and_storage.the_compounding_honesty: We cannot fly in the storm AND we cannot charge in the overcast. Two independent limitations pointing the same direction, during the season that matters most. A fixed buoy has neither problem. This is the strongest technical argument for the buoy hybrid that our own competitive matrix already pointed at, and it should be confronted rather than buried in a solar sizing appendix.

dock_network_economics.the_unit: One dock covers the stations within the aircraft's operating radius. A large lake needs several.
dock_network_economics.the_implication: Deployment cost scales with docks, which scales with water body area and geometry — not with the number of cages or the customer's revenue. A big, sparse lake is expensive to cover and generates the same revenue per cage as a small dense one.
dock_network_economics.the_strategic_consequence: Dense cage clusters are dramatically better unit economics than dispersed ones. This should be a hard sales qualification criterion, and it independently reinforces the lake-cage beachhead choice over dispersed pond geography. The geography chooses the customer.
dock_network_economics.the_cooperative_synergy: A dock serving forty co-op members' cages around one bay is the best economics available to this business. A dock serving one farm is the worst. This is another reason the cooperative channel matters beyond the affordability argument.

requirements.statement: The dock shall launch and recover the aircraft without human presence.
requirements.note: Regulatory permission for unattended operation is a separate matter — see regulatory-framework.json. The dock being capable and the operation being legal are different questions.

requirements.statement: The dock shall rinse the sonde and the full deployed length of the tether after every water sortie.
requirements.rationale: The tether is a contamination and fouling path that is routinely overlooked.

requirements.statement: The dock shall house a calibrated reflectance reference imaged before and after every imaging sortie.
The dock shall maintain internal temperature below the battery's safe storage limit under peak local insolation.
requirements.rationale: Silent battery degradation is an operating cost discovered a year late.

requirements.statement: The dock shall store not less than 7 days of sortie data locally and forward it when connectivity returns.
requirements.rationale: The link will fail. The pre-dawn profile must survive it.

requirements.statement: The dock shall operate on solar with storage sufficient for 3 consecutive days of overcast at reduced cadence.
The dock shall be serviceable by a technician with hand tools.
The dock shall detect and report its own faults, including a failure to open.
requirements.rationale: A dock that silently fails to open at 04:30 produces a missing dawn profile that nobody notices until it matters. Silent failure of the monitoring system is the worst failure mode a monitoring system has.

open_questions.question: Recharge or swap?
open_questions.why: Determines sortie cadence, dock complexity, and cost.

open_questions.question: What decontaminant, at what contact time, against which pathogens?
open_questions.why: It is a biosecurity claim, and claims need evidence. This needs an aquatic animal health specialist, not a founder's judgement.

open_questions.question: Does the dock survive a typhoon, or is it removed?
open_questions.why: A permanent lakeshore structure in the Philippines must answer this. If it is removed seasonally, the deployment model changes.

open_questions.question: Who owns the shore, and what does that cost?
open_questions.why: Land tenure is not an engineering problem and it will still stop a deployment.
